replies.author: @robertbeard230
replies.text:
Yes, husband of Percy Shelley. I suspect two things…

First, much of Percy Shelley’s poetry wasn’t published until after his death by his grieving widow Mary Shelley. I suspect she actually wrote a most of it and published under her husband’s name. An awful lot of that poetry sounds feminine, even from a historical perspective; cases in particular include “A Vision of the Sea” and “Remembrance”.

The second thing I suspect is that Edgar Allen Poe, who was 9 when Mary Shelley published that book of (supposedly) her husbands poetry, copied nearly verbatim a stanza of Remembrance and used it as a jumping off point to write The Raven. 

Anyone who doubts either of these can investigate it themselves. 

That said, there are descriptions of blood and gore in “A Vision of the Sea” that sound an awful lot like the macabre imagery in Frankenstein, as originally written by Mary Shelley.

author: @lucylee59
text: I have an idea for a GREAT movie.. What would have happened if the left had treated Trump with honesty? IF they had not called him a racist, not lied about the friendships they have had with them for years. What if the media praised him and the view showed all his great accomplishments? How do you think the world would look right now? Seriously, the whole movie set that we are living in was created by the media. They set the stage, filled it with lies, and only showed the scenes that proved their point when manipulated. I just wonder where we would be.
